title: Do not use toast notifications for every system message
summary: AI-generated interfaces universally use the four-color toast notification (green/red/blue/yellow) appearing top-right with auto-dismiss for every system message, from trivial saves to critical errors.
instruction: Match notification persistence and prominence to message urgency. Reserve toasts for passive, non-critical confirmations. Use persistent banners or inline states for messages requiring user action. Do not show success toasts for operations the user can see succeeded (inline saves, applied filters).
rationale: The universal toast notification trains users to ignore all notifications. Success toasts on every saved-change operation create notification fatigue. The 3-second auto-dismiss fails readers who need more time, and the top-right position competes with browser-native notifications.
violations: Four-color toast system: green success, red error, blue info, yellow warning
Auto-dismiss at approximately 3 seconds regardless of message length
Success toast for every save, update, or create operation
Dismiss X button on every toast even when auto-dismissing
controls: Inline success state replacing the trigger element (e.g., button text changes to 'Saved')
Persistent banner at top of page for messages requiring user action
Subtle inline confirmation without a toast overlay
No confirmation at all for operations the user initiated and can see the result of
borderlineCases: Messages that need to reach the user regardless of page context
contextualExceptions: Real-time collaboration notifications that must appear on any page
detectorSignal: A small positioned rectangle (top-right or top-center) appearing temporarily with slide-in animation, containing an icon, short text, and optional close button.
matcherProposal: Detect positioned elements with slide-in animations, colored backgrounds (green/red/blue/yellow), containing an icon and text, that auto-dismiss.
detectorLimitations: Cannot determine if the notification urgency matches the toast format
falsePositiveScenarios: Applications where every user action genuinely needs confirmation feedback
mitigationStrategy: Flag success toasts on operations that have visible inline effects more aggressively than error or warning toasts.

fixturePlans.violation: Green success toast 'Profile saved!' appearing after every profile form save, auto-dismissing 3 seconds.
fixturePlans.control: Save button text changes to 'Saved!' briefly, no toast overlay, color returns to normal after 2 seconds.
fixturePlans.borderline: Critical error toast for payment failure that persists until dismissed.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
